title: io.podman.annotations.volumes-from doesn't work in 4.9.3
body:
### Issue Description

__!!! BEFORE YOU CONTINUE !!!__
I did check the code and the annotation is present in the 4.9.3 branch, but that doesn't mean that I'm right ^-^
To avoid wasting anyone's time just stop here and close the issue if the feature is not present in 4.9.3, thank you.

---

Running containers with kube manifests and shared volume mounts (volumes-from) is not working in 4.9.3 using the `io.podman.annotations.volumes-from` annotation. Running on openSUSE MicroOS. Rootless or rootfull, with or without SELinux I get the same result. Checked the build source on OBS, and the annotation is present.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Use the [same manifest](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/42a78c714c48012672b920b724f7e402b0c901ca/test/e2e/play_kube_test.go#L495) as the e2e tests do in the _Podman_ repo
2. Set the images to `docker.io/alpine` as an example
3. Start the pod with `podman kube play`
4. Check the contents on `/mnt` of the `tgtctr` container: `podman exec -it volspod-tgtctr ls /mnt`

### Describe the results you received

The volume is present in the `srcctr` container, but not in the `tgtctr` container.

### Describe the results you expected

The shared volume is present in both containers.

comments.body: This was just merged and will only land in 5.0 https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/42a78c714c48012672b920b724f7e402b0c901ca
